warn_repo_zero_packages: WARNING: {0} returned 0 packages (check pacman.conf architecture setting)
error_no_operation: error: no operation specified (use -h for help)
error_need_root: error: you must be root to perform this operation
help_text:
usage:  pacman <operation>[...]

operations:
    pacman {-h --help}
    pacman {-V --version}
    pacman {-D --database} <options> <package(s)>
    pacman {-F --files}    [options] [file(s)]
    pacman {-Q --query}    [options] [package(s)]
    pacman {-R --remove}   [options] <package(s)>
    pacman {-S --sync}     [options] [package(s)]
    pacman {-T --deptest}  [options] [package(s)]
    pacman {-U --upgrade}  [options] <file(s)>

use 'pacman {-h --help}' with an operation for available options
help_S:
usage:  pacman {-S --sync} [options] [package(s)]

options:
  -c, --clean          remove old packages from cache directory (-cc for all)
  -i, --info           view package information
  -l, --list <repo>    view a list of packages in a repo
  -p, --print          print urls of packages
  -s, --search <regex> search remote repositories for matching strings
  -u, --sysupgrade     upgrade installed packages
  -w, --downloadonly   download packages but do not install/upgrade anything
  -y, --refresh        download fresh package databases from the server
                       (-yy to force a refresh even if up to date)
      --asdeps         install packages as non-explicitly installed
      --needed         do not reinstall up to date packages
help_U:
usage:  pacman {-U --upgrade} [options] <file(s)>

options:
      --needed         do not reinstall up to date packages
help_R:
usage:  pacman {-R --remove} [options] <package(s)>

options:
  -c, --cascade        remove packages and all packages that depend on them
  -d, --nodeps         skip dependency version checks
  -n, --nosave         remove configuration files
  -p, --print          print the targets instead of performing the operation
  -s, --recursive      remove unnecessary dependencies
help_Q:
usage:  pacman {-Q --query} [options] [package(s)]

options:
  -d, --deps           list packages installed as dependencies [filter]
  -e, --explicit       list packages explicitly installed [filter]
  -i, --info           view package information
  -k, --check          check that package files exist (-kk for verbose)
  -l, --list           list the files owned by the queried package
  -o, --owns <file>    query the package that owns <file>
  -q, --quiet          show less information for query and search
  -s, --search <regex> search locally-installed packages for matching strings
  -dt                  list packages not required by any package [filter]
help_D:
usage:  pacman {-D --database} <options> <package(s)>

options:
      --asdeps         mark packages as non-explicitly installed
      --asexplicit     mark packages as explicitly installed
help_F:
usage:  pacman {-F --files} [options] [file(s)]

options:
  -y, --refresh        download fresh package databases from the server
                       (not maintained by pacman-debian)
help_global:
global options:
  -b, --dbpath <path>  set an alternate database location
  -r, --root <path>    set an alternate installation root
  -v, --verbose        be verbose
      --color <when>   colorize the output
      --config <path>  set an alternate configuration file
      --debug          display debug messages
      --noconfirm      do not ask for any confirmation
      --confirm        always ask for confirmation
      --noprogressbar  do not show a progress bar when downloading files
      --noscriptlet    do not execute the install scriptlet if one exists
warn_failed_dpkg_status: WARNING: failed to write dpkg status: {0}
warn_arch_mismatch: warning: system arch {0} not in PKGBUILD arch list, using {1}
info_install_reason_dep: Install Reason : Installed as a dependency (pacman-debian)
setup_need_root: This script needs root to create symlinks and config files.
setup_step_4: 4. Run makepkg in a directory with PKGBUILD to build packages
setup_step_5: 5. For AUR helper support, build and install libalpm (re-run this script)
source_takeover_warning: warning: {0} currently comes from {1}; {2} will take over its files and package record.
error_no_build_function: PKGBUILD has no build(), prepare(), check(), or package() function
